informal_statement.text: Given a sorted slice of integers in nondecreasing order and a target value, return Some(i) where arr[i] == target, or None if the target is absent. If multiple indices match, returning any valid index is acceptable.
preconditions: arr is sorted in nondecreasing order
required_properties: if the function returns Some(i), then i < arr.len() and arr[i] == target
if the function returns None, then target does not occur in arr
the function terminates for every input
edge_cases: single-element slice not containing the target

semantic_units.description: Input precondition: the slice is sorted in nondecreasing order.
common_failure_modes: asserting strict ordering instead of nondecreasing

semantic_units.description: Success postcondition: if the return is Some(i), then i is a valid index and arr[i] == target.
Failure postcondition: if the return is None, then target does not occur in arr.
common_failure_modes: only quantifying over a prefix or suffix
stating the weaker claim that target is not at a specific position

semantic_units.description: Termination: the function terminates on every input (lo..hi shrinks each iteration).
Uniqueness is NOT required: multiple valid indices exist when duplicates are present.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr) (t : Int) (i : Nat), binarySearch arr t = some i → i < arr.length ∧ arr.get? i = some t
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the function returns Some i, then i is in range and arr[i] equals the target.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr) (t : Int), SortedLE arr → binarySearch arr t = none → ∀ i, i < arr.length → arr.get? i ≠ some t
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: Assuming the input is sorted in nondecreasing order, if the function returns None then the target does not occur anywhere in the slice.

generated_obligations.nl_gloss: No explicit precondition is asserted by the implementation. The only required property for correctness is that the input slice is sorted nondecreasing, which will be captured in later obligations.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int), (∀ i j : Fin arr.length, i ≤ j → arr[i] ≤ arr[j]) → True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the array is sorted in nondecreasing order: for any indices i ≤ j, arr[i] ≤ arr[j].

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), ∀ (lo hi : Nat), lo ≤ hi ∧ hi ≤ arr.length → True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: During the loop, maintain index bounds: 0 ≤ lo ≤ hi ≤ arr.length.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (lo hi : Nat), lo ≤ hi → hi ≤ arr.length → (∀ k : Fin lo, arr[k] < target) → True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Loop invariant: all indices strictly less than lo cannot contain the target (their values are < target).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (lo hi : Nat), lo ≤ hi → hi ≤ arr.length → (∀ k : Fin (arr.length - hi), arr[⟨hi + k.1, by omega⟩] > target) → True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Loop invariant: all indices ≥ hi cannot contain the target (their values are > target).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), (∀ i j : Fin arr.length, i ≤ j → arr[i] ≤ arr[j]) → (let lo := 0; let hi := arr.length; (lo ≤ hi) ∧ (hi ≤ arr.length) ∧ (∀ k : Fin lo, arr[k] < target) ∧ (∀ k : Fin (arr.length - hi), arr[⟨hi + k.1, by omega⟩] > target))
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: At loop start, lo = 0 and hi = arr.length. The exclusion invariants hold vacuously.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (lo hi : Nat) (arr : Array Int), lo < hi → let mid := lo + (hi - lo) / 2; lo ≤ mid ∧ mid < hi
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: When lo < hi, the computed mid lies within [lo, hi) so arr[mid] is a valid access and the search interval shrinks correctly.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (lo hi mid : Nat), lo ≤ mid ∧ mid < hi → arr[mid] = target → True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If arr[mid] = target, the algorithm returns Some(mid). This obligation is a trivial correctness step for the found case.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (lo hi mid : Nat), lo ≤ mid ∧ mid < hi → arr[mid] < target → (∀ k : Fin lo, arr[k] < target) → (∀ k : Fin (arr.length - hi), arr[⟨hi + k.1, by omega⟩] > target) → let lo' := mid + 1; let hi' := hi; (lo' ≤ hi') ∧ (∀ k : Fin lo', arr[k] < target) ∧ (∀ k : Fin (arr.length - hi'), arr[⟨hi' + k.1, by omega⟩] > target)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If arr[mid] < target, update lo := mid+1. The left exclusion invariant strengthens to include mid, and the right exclusion invariant remains unchanged.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (lo hi mid : Nat), lo ≤ mid ∧ mid < hi → arr[mid] > target → (∀ k : Fin lo, arr[k] < target) → (∀ k : Fin (arr.length - hi), arr[⟨hi + k.1, by omega⟩] > target) → let lo' := lo; let hi' := mid; (lo' ≤ hi') ∧ (∀ k : Fin lo', arr[k] < target) ∧ (∀ k : Fin (arr.length - hi'), arr[⟨hi' + k.1, by omega⟩] > target)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If arr[mid] > target, update hi := mid. The right exclusion invariant strengthens to include mid, and the left exclusion invariant remains unchanged.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), ∀ (lo hi : Nat), lo ≤ hi → (hi - lo) decreases strictly on each loop iteration when lo < hi
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove termination: the interval length (hi - lo) strictly decreases each iteration, so the loop eventually reaches lo = hi.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (i : Nat), (∀ i' j' : Fin arr.length, i' ≤ j' → arr[i'] ≤ arr[j']) → (binary_search arr target = some i) → i < arr.length ∧ arr[i] = target
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the function returns Some(i), then i is within bounds and arr[i] equals target.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int), (∀ i j : Fin arr.length, i ≤ j → arr[i] ≤ arr[j]) → (binary_search arr target = none) → (∀ k : Fin arr.length, arr[k] ≠ target)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the function returns None, then the target does not occur anywhere in the array.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (i : Nat), (∀ i' j' : Fin arr.length, i' ≤ j' → arr[i'] ≤ arr[j']) → (binary_search arr target = some i) → (∃ k : Fin arr.length, k.1 = i ∧ arr[k] = target)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the function returns Some(i), then there exists an index in the array where the value equals target (i.e., membership).
